title: prunus serotina subsp. eximia
intro: Prunus serotina subsp. eximia, also known as the Ozark black cherry, is a subspecies of the black cherry tree native to the Ozark Mountains in North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Prunus serotina subsp. eximia include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Prunus serotina subsp. eximia can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tinctures, teas, and capsules, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
